area: Protected staged bundle / Broker release-order deadlock
finding: A Broker resolution can correctly grant task A the current shared-surface release order, but A's governed commit cannot use `--defer-foreign-staged` when task B's protected `.atm/history` bundle is staged. B cannot commit first because the same resolution blocks B.
expectedBehavior: A resolution-authorized current task must be able to snapshot and temporarily unstage a complete protected foreign task bundle, commit only its own scope, then return a deterministic restage command; incomplete or non-resolution foreign bundles remain protected.
evidenceOrRepro: Closeout audit after TASK-TEAM-0079 and TASK-TEAM-0080 found the stale `Active in TASK-TEAM-0079` status was incorrect: 0079 closed queue/composer projection issues, and 0080 propagated `--defer-foreign-staged` through taskflow plus scope tools, but `git-governance.ts` still blocks protected foreign staged ownership with `ATM_GIT_COMMIT_PROTECTED_FOREIGN_STAGED_OWNERSHIP`.
followUp: TASK-TEAM-0084 records the remaining boundary as follow-up: implement or reject a narrow resolution-authorized protected defer lane with regression proving no silent unstage, complete-bundle validation, snapshot/recovery, and denial without currentAllowedTaskId.
